REMARK:HEARING HELD FOR EX PARTE MOTION FOR RELIEF TO (1) PERMIT PLAINTIFF LISA MOSCHETTI TO FILE A SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. FIRST, THE COURT DID NOT ADVANCE THE DEMURRER TO THE AMENDED COMPLAINT; RATHER, IT WAS CONTINUED FROM DECEMBER 16, 2025, TO DECEMBER 29, 2025. SECOND, BOTH PARTIES FAILED TO COMPLY WITH THE COURT'S RULING AT THE DECEMBER 29, 2025 HEARING, AS NO PROPOSED ORDER WAS SUBMITTED TO THE COURT. THIRD, THE MOVING PARTY FAILED TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WITH ITS EX PARTE APPLICATION AS REQUIRED BY CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT, RULE 3.1201(5). ACCORDINGLY, THE COURT DENIED THE APPLICATION. (302/JMQ)

David Amirian, Esq. 310-289-9100 Law Offices of Sharona Eslamboly Hakim for Plaintiff.
Rebecca Fisher, Esq. 628-253-5070 Tyson & Mendes for Defendant.

ARGUED.
Ms. Fisher contests the TR on the 4th COA and 5th COA.
Court comments.
Mr. Amirian agrees with the TR and has nothing to add.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-26-2026 Continued To Jun-09-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-15-2026 Continued To Apr-07-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-29-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances in Open Court. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendants The One Group, LLC and STK Steakhouse's Demurrer to Amended Complaint is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND.

The court takes Plaintiff's "Notice of Second Amended Complaint in Opposition" as an opposition seeking leave to amend. The court, thus, sustains the demurrer and grants Plaintiff leave to amend by filing a Second Amended Complaint. Any alleged deficiencies in the SAC can be addressed after Plaintiff files the SAC.

Plaintiff is admonished that she must fulfill her duties to confer with opposing counsel as appropriate. Defendants' counsel reports efforts to confer with Plaintiff regarding the demurrer, as required by the Legislature. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 430.41.) Plaintiff failed to fulfill her duty to discuss the issues with the other parties. The Legislature has imposed a duty to meet and confer on all parties. Meet and confer is especially important in connection with scheduling orders, anticipated demurrers, motions to strike and all discovery disagreements. Plaintiff is ordered to meet and confer with the other parties to this litigation as required by the Legislature.

Plaintiff must file her Second Amended Complaint no later that January 15, 2026.
